# Jobs to be done

([[Fabiano Dalpiaz 2018]])

- In den Nutzenden hineinversetzen um seine Bedürfnisse zu verstehen
	- Wie fühlt er sich?
		- Ist er gelangweilt/beschäftigt/gestresst/entspannt?
	- Wo ist er gerade?
		- Ist seine Umgebung hell/dunkel/schnell/langsam?
	- Was würde er gerade lieber machen?
- Aufbau: Sätze formulieren, die seinen Job beinhalten und das Gefühl, dass er dabei haben möchte
	- As a lab tech I want (Job to be done) so that (**what in here?**)
		- Das **Gefühl** oder so?	


1.  As a lab tech I want to place samples so that I don't have to think about them until end of day
	- 1st thing
2. As a lab tech I want a computer to encode my samples so that I don't waste my time.
	- Alone
3. As a lab tech I want to control which samples are being picked so that I don't waste my time.
	- Lab, tiny, slow paced environment 
4. As a lab tech I want not not manually process samples so that I can sit in front of my computer and be productive.
	- change location, at his desk 
5. As a lab tech I want to trust Wandelbots solutions for automated pipetting so that I have another hour per day for a wider variety of tasks; I don't want to become hyperspecialized.
	- in a meeting, focused
6. As a lab tech I want to make nothing interrupt my robot so that I truly recharge my batteries during lunch time in my favorite restaurant.
	- lunch break, walking somewhere, noon, no phone, hungry
7. As a lab tech I want to have emptied the bin already before starting my work so that I work efficiently because a fluent automation process.
	-   busy
